arguments.command:
cd /tmp/sv-067783637bdb474680b77caa5558b4e5
BUILDID="4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801"

# Create a minimal ELF stub with the correct build ID so dotnet-symbol can use it
python3 -c "
import struct

# Minimal ELF64 with a GNU build ID note
# ELF header
e_ident = b'\x7fELF'  # magic
e_ident += b'\x02'     # 64-bit
e_ident += b'\x01'     # little endian
e_ident += b'\x01'     # ELF version
e_ident += b'\x00' * 9 # padding
e_class = 2   # 64-bit
e_data = 1    # little endian

buildid_hex = '$BUILDID'
buildid_bytes = bytes.fromhex(buildid_hex)
name = b'GNU\x00'
# Note header: namesz, descsz, type
note_hdr = struct.pack('<III', len(name), len(buildid_bytes), 3)  # NT_GNU_BUILD_ID = 3
note_data = note_hdr + name + buildid_bytes

# Pad note to 4-byte alignment
while len(note_data) % 4:
    note_data += b'\x00'

# ELF header (64 bytes) + program header (56 bytes)  
ehdr_size = 64
phdr_size = 56
note_offset = ehdr_size + phdr_size

# ELF header
ehdr = b'\x7fELF'           # magic
ehdr += b'\x02'              # 64-bit
ehdr += b'\x01'              # little-endian  
ehdr += b'\x01'              # version
ehdr += b'\x03'              # OS/ABI: Linux
ehdr += b'\x00' * 8          # padding
ehdr += struct.pack('<H', 3) # ET_DYN (shared object)
ehdr += struct.pack('<H', 0xB7) # EM_AARCH64
ehdr += struct.pack('<I', 1) # version
ehdr += struct.pack('<Q', 0) # entry point
ehdr += struct.pack('<Q', ehdr_size) # program header offset
ehdr += struct.pack('<Q', 0) # section header offset
ehdr += struct.pack('<I', 0) # flags
ehdr += struct.pack('<H', ehdr_size) # ELF header size
ehdr += struct.pack('<H', phdr_size) # program header entry size
ehdr += struct.pack('<H', 1) # number of program headers
ehdr += struct.pack('<H', 0) # section header entry size
ehdr += struct.pack('<H', 0) # number of section headers
ehdr += struct.pack('<H', 0) # section name string table index

# Program header (PT_NOTE)
phdr = struct.pack('<I', 4)          # PT_NOTE
phdr += struct.pack('<I', 0)         # flags
phdr += struct.pack('<Q', note_offset) # offset
phdr += struct.pack('<Q', note_offset) # vaddr
phdr += struct.pack('<Q', note_offset) # paddr
phdr += struct.pack('<Q', len(note_data)) # filesz
phdr += struct.pack('<Q', len(note_data)) # memsz
phdr += struct.pack('<Q', 4)         # align

with open('libcoreclr.so', 'wb') as f:
    f.write(ehdr + phdr + note_data)

print(f'Created stub ELF with build ID {buildid_hex}')
print(f'File size: {ehdr_size + phdr_size + len(note_data)} bytes')
"

# Verify the build ID
file libcoreclr.so
readelf -n libcoreclr.so 2>&1 || echo "readelf not available for this arch"

# Download a few .NET 10 Android ARM64 runtime packages and check build IDs
# Start with versions around Feb 2026 timeframe
for ver in 10.0.2 10.0.3 10.0.4 10.0.5; do
  echo "=== Checking version $ver ==="
  URL="https://api.nuget.org/v3-flatcontainer/microsoft.netcore.app.runtime.android-arm64/${ver}/microsoft.netcore.app.runtime.android-arm64.${ver}.nupkg"
  # Download just enough to find libcoreclr.so
  cd /tmp/nuget_check

  if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    # Extract only libcoreclr.so
    unzip -q -o "pkg_${ver}.nupkg" "runtimes/android-arm64/native/libcoreclr.so" -d "extracted_${ver}" 2>/dev/null
    CORECLR="extracted_${ver}/runtimes/android-arm64/native/libcoreclr.so"
    if [ -f "$CORECLR" ]; then
      BUILDID=$(readelf -n "$CORECLR" 2>/dev/null | grep "Build ID:" | awk '{print $3}')
      echo "  Build ID: $BUILDID"
      SIZE=$(stat -c%s "$CORECLR")
      echo "  Size: $SIZE bytes"
    else
      echo "  libcoreclr.so not found in package"
      # List what's in the package
      unzip -l "pkg_${ver}.nupkg" 2>/dev/null | grep -i "coreclr" | head -5
    fi
  else
    echo "  Download failed"
  fi
  rm -f "pkg_${ver}.nupkg"
done
